Welcome everyone to the second style of quality app video ads. And so I'm stealing the term educate from a good friend, Tom Breeze, who runs a YouTube video ad agency. So all he does is video ads and he spends millions of dollars a month in video ads in all different types of niches. And he has boiled down his formula to this. And I have adopted this formula in what we do for RB2B stuff. And I want to be very transparent with you about some of the differences. And so I like to always tell everyone, like, I always like to show my cards. And so I have B2B educate style videos that are three, four, five, six minutes long. And they work for us. I think that part of that, and I'm not going to recommend everyone do that because most of the people who are watching this video are going to be fitness oriented. And so B2B business owners in a niche are going to have a very different expectation and different attention spans related to value in a video than somebody who's trying to lose weight. And so we need to be faster, a little bit more condensed in this video style. And so I want to just show the overall structure and then I will try and I will apply it given the conversations I've had with three different people who are spending over a million dollars a month in fitness video ads. And so that is where the consolidated findings for this are. And so that's I just want to kind of set this as setting the tone for that. So I'm going to be introducing their frameworks consolidated with the things that I found best for our ads that I feel like apply to not just B2B, but just, you know, selling in general. I want to be clear here that value of the actual video, the actual content itself is incredibly important. So making sure that someone finds something useful and tactically actionable that they can actually take something away that was valuable from the video is going to be the primary driver of whether or not it works. Okay. And the second thing is that your personality on the video is going to matter more than it does in a typical direct selling ad. And so I want to be clear that a lot of people don't succeed not because of the structure, but because either the thing they're trying to teach is not valuable or perceived as valuable or their personality on video sucks. And so the last point I'm going to make here is that like, you're like, well, I'm not good on video, well, then you need to become more skilled. And that's because the people who have the most skills are the ones who get paid the most because they're the most skilled. And so I'm just being candid with you like that is what it is. And that's why I still almost hesitated to bring this style up, but in being truthful about like how we advertise and how the best to advertise, a lot of them use this style of video. Okay. And I want this to be a complete training. And so I would be remiss to not include this style of video and the structure that I'm going to lay out for you. All right. So the one adaptations that's different than what we do is that you should have a minute, 30 second long style of videos for fitness. The highest I've seen is two minutes. Lois is about a minute. So it's going to be between the one and two minute mark shoot for 90 seconds and you should be good to go. Now you'll notice that 90 seconds to cover all the things that I have, I'm going to show you is not a lot of time. And that's why you have to be incredibly deliberate with the words that you're saying to make sure that no words are added to create a video that is just pure value. So here's the educate video structure. And I'm going to relate it to something that everyone in our community knows in a moment. So aim is simply their goal. Right? Why are they actually watching this video? What are they seeking to understand? The next is the difficulty that they have. So hey, you know, for watching this video, you're probably trying to get in shape, you know, etc. and you've had this problem. X, Y, Z. You can't stay consistent with it. You can't stay with it. You can't stick with the diet. You can't stick with the working out. Right? So you want to name the difficulties and the more specific you can be to the difficulty. Like saying you can't eat well versus like you're always guessing about what you're going to have for dinner every night. One is more specific and that's going to be more relatable. So the more creative you can be in how you phrase, which is what copy is, the more it will sink in and resonate with the person. All right? So what's their goal? What's the difficulty that they're having? You want to show that you understand that difficulty. Like I totally get it. I used to be the same way when I lost my hundred and pounds, blah, blah, blah, blah. Right? And so like right there, we're like, we got the goal. We hit the difficulty of that they're experiencing the obstacle that's in their way and that you are offering understanding. Now at this point in the video from a subconscious level, the person, if you hit each of these points well and you really, there's actually something that's subconscious. When someone can state the problem that you're having or the way that you're feeling better than you can, you subconsciously trust them. When someone articulates how you feel better than you can articulate it, you trust them because you're like, wow, they really know what I'm feeling, feeling being the keyword here. All right? And so they want to trust you, but there's one small part of them that's like, but what if they don't know what they're talking about? And so you want to slip in. I mean, and I'm saying slipping in one to two sentences of credibility. So this is what we've done at my six facilities and we've helped over a thousand women in the last year in this local market. Boom. And then you transition. And just like that, we've captured their attention with the pain. So this is what you want. This is the problem that you're having and I understand because XY, because I've gone through it too. Or because I've helped, because I went through it and I've seen over 1500 people who've gone through it with me at my gym XYZ. And so just like that, in that short introduction, that might have only been 25 seconds of the minute and a half. We've already hit these first four. All right? Next is going to be the action plan. This is where you're going to explain to them the three steps or the five steps or whatever that you're going to make for them that they can find value from in the video. So the reason you haven't had that is because you haven't had fitness that you've actually enjoyed doing. Right? If you look forward to your fitness, like you look forward to Netflix, you don't really struggle to watch Netflix, right? Of course not. It's because you actually like it. So we just need to figure out a way for you to do that. And here's a way that we figured out that worked well. And it needs to be tactical and useful to the end user. And this is where if you have a particular style or dogma from a marketing standpoint for your fitness or your nutrition, this is where you'd want to put that in. So this is where like, yeah, and the reason most people struggle is they can't stay consistent with meals because they're always obsessed with eating all these snacks and you can never get your timing right. And so that's why when I figured out intermittent fasting, boom, right, you've broken that one belief. And so the objectives that we're trying to break one single belief in this video, you're not trying to sell everything. You're not selling the dream. You're breaking one belief in the video. All right. And so pick the belief that you want. Like, you know what? It was the issue is I just couldn't stop dealing with sugar. And so I think I found that the ketogenic diet, I was able to do it. Now mind you, be true to you. If you don't believe in that, then don't market it. But I'm just telling you from a marketing perspective, you need to have a widget that's going to be different or unique. Okay. Then you're going to teach that one thing. So you introduce the like the reason, the three reasons that blah, blah, blah, blah. And then you'll teach like the last piece is something that they can immediately use. All right. That has to be valuable and tactical. And then the exit is just your call to action to get them to do the next thing. Right. So I want to relate this back to what we've already know from our closure sequence, but it's essentially selling. Right. So we're selling, it's disguised selling. So you're clarifying the goal, you're labeling the problem, which is the difficulty, your understanding, you're going over their past pain of what they've experienced. You add in your single line of credibility for yourself. You'll sell the solution. The three pillars are what you're going to teach. And then you'll have your oops, one useful nugget and then a CTA. All right. So that is the overarching overview. And I just wanted to tie it because I want everyone to understand big picture here. If you're trying to learn strategy or trying to learn how to market, selling is always the same. Right. And so this is the sequence that always what it is. Why are they there? Here's the problem they're having overview, why they haven't been able to experience the results in the past. Right. And then you're giving yourself a line of credibility. This is because of online marketing space. You need to have some credibility. So this is where B roll. This is where a picture of you in front of your entire gym, like everyone cheering, you showing like your, well, you can't show testimonials on, on, on a lot of advertising. Well, on Facebook, you can't at least, but on different advertising platforms, that's where you put, you know, a foreign afters and things like that. And then you'll sell the solution to why this, why people find you credible. And you're saying, this is my new process. You give them one useful nugget they can walk away with. And if you like this and you want to find out more, should click here and look at our, you know, 12 week transformation program or six week or whatever. And it's absolutely free. Right. So that concludes the overview of the educate style video. This is the sequence. It should be a minute and a half total. That's really what you should shoot for this. You're essentially selling with more clarity and you want to be more deliberate in the scripting of the video.
